content:
Two people live together. No marriage. No paperwork. So what happens if things go wrong?
Most assume the law looks the other way. That’s not entirely true.
In reality, live-in relationships are not “illegal” in India. And in certain cases, the law does step in for protection. The biggest misconception is that there is zero legal recognition. Courts especially in India have gradually expanded protections through judicial interpretation not direct legislation.
Courts have held that long-term live-in partners can be covered under domestic violence protections if the relationship resembles a marriage-like setup. That can include protection from abuse and even right to shared residence. But there’s a catch: casual or short-term arrangements may not qualify. Proof of cohabitation matters. In some cases, partners may also seek maintenance-like support depending on dependency and duration of relationship.
Do live-in relationships deserve clearer legal recognition, or does current law already strike the right balance?
